excerpts:
Section Title: Tamara Harris > About
Content:
As an editorial intern, I work amongst my team researching topics weekly, creating a team effort article that will be published onto Mind4Youth’s website, and making digital posters for the media in order to bring attention to mental health.

title: March 17 | Tamara Winfrey Harris Discusses Dear Black Girl at MahoganyBooks — ANACOSTIA BID
excerpts:
Section Title: March 17 | Tamara Winfrey Harris Discusses Dear Black Girl at MahoganyBooks
Content:
March 17, 2021 at 7:00pm
[**REGISTER HERE**](https://www.crowdcast.io/e/tamara-winfrey-harris/register?utm_campaign=profile&utm_medium=profile_web&utm_source=profile)
Join MahoganyBooks Front Row for a special evening of authentic exchanges between  Tamara Winfrey Harris and Faith Adiele as they discuss Dear Black Girl:  Letters from Your Sisters on Stepping Into Your Power, Tamara's latest work. [**Grab your copy today!**](https://www.mahoganybooks.com/9781523092291)
**The Book:** In Dear Black Girl, Winfrey-Harris introduces and organizes a selection of collected letters from all over the world, modeling how black women can nurture future generations. Each chapter ends with a prompt encouraging girls to write a letter to themselves, teaching the art of self-love and self-nurturing.
